title: Intense Mustardy Thai Pear Soup
description: A spicy, buttery‑laced tom yum soup that pairs earthy Korean pear with roasted tomato and a bold mustard kick, all wrapped in a dairy‑free coconut broth. Its airy, comforting broth is perfect for a quick winter lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: tomatoes
ingredients.amount: 3 medium, roasted with 1 tablespoon olive oil

ingredients.name: mustard powder
ingredients.notes: toast for 30 seconds to release flavor

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and roast tomatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper for 20 minutes until caramelized.
In a large pot, combine vegetable broth and coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the sliced lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, roasted tomatoes, and diced Korean pear to the pot and simmer for 8 minutes.
Stir in mustard powder, lime zest, lime juice, soy sauce, and chili paste, letting the flavors meld for 5 minutes.
Remove lemongrass stalks and lime leaves with a slotted spoon, then discard.
Use an immersion blender to purée the soup until smooth, adding a splash of water if needed for desired consistency.
Taste the soup and adjust seasoning with additional lime juice or soy sauce as preferred.
Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish generously with fresh cilantro leaves.
Serve hot, offering a comforting and mustard‑infused Thai experience.
Allow the soup to rest for 2 minutes before eating to let the aromatics settle.
